pi_items.source: Verified across task.json description, setup_task.sh (line 55), export_result.sh (line 39), README.md (lines 15, 59), and verifier.py. The room name is used consistently in Firefox navigation URL: http://localhost:8080/Q4ExecutiveBoard

Verified across task.json description (line 5), README.md (line 17), export_result.sh (line 41) which searches for 'password|Board2024|locked...', and verifier.py comments (line 111). Password is also referenced in verifier.py scoring rules.

Verified across task.json description (line 5), task.json metadata (line 18), setup_task.sh (line 49) which removes any pre-existing file, export_result.sh (line 22), and README.md (line 19). Verifier.py reads report metadata via export_result.sh.

Verified across task.json description (line 5), setup_jitsi.sh (lines 78, 84) confirming service runs at http://localhost:8080, docker-compose.yml (line 25) setting PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:8080, and task_utils.sh (line 4) setting JITSI_BASE_URL. The docker-compose uses jitsi/web:stable-9753 image mapped to ports 8080:80.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task-specific scenario simulating a real-world security operations task. All metadata values are verified as correct and consistently implemented across task configuration files. The task uses Jitsi Meet stable-9753 running in Docker at http://localhost:8080. The specific room name (Q4ExecutiveBoard), password (Board2024!), and report path (/home/ga/Desktop/board_security_summary.txt) are invented test values chosen by the task designer to create a realistic operations manager scenario involving meeting security configuration (lobby and password features). The Jitsi version supports both lobby mode (for manual participant approval) and password protection, confirming that the task scenario is technically feasible with the configured Jitsi setup.
